timeline.event: agent-runtime v2.3.1 deployed: async completion reporting optimization, updated status transition logic
completed_without_artifact metric begins rising from 0.2/min baseline
On-call engineer Alex picks up alert, initially suspects storage latency
Status-ordering bug identified: tasks transition to completed without actual artifact write
Anomaly peak: 6.2/min completed_without_artifact, 33/min total completions, write latency p99 at 2100ms
T-2003: Pilot Bank A compliance team flags 8 empty reports, customer escalation
Engineering confirms v2.3.1 deployment correlation — async write with no success check
Customer escalation email from Sarah Chen, VP Compliance Pilot Bank A: 8+ empty reports, SLA concern
v2.3.2 hotfix deployed to all 8 nodes, reverting async completion changes
Alerts cleared: all metrics return to baseline
Post-hotfix verification: all nodes healthy, three sample runs confirmed

confirmed_facts: v2.3.1 was deployed at 09:15 UTC on 2025-06-12 (deployment-log.md, system-metrics.txt timing correlation)
The completed_without_artifact anomaly began at 09:15 UTC and subsided after the 11:30 UTC hotfix (system-metrics.txt, team-notes.md)
The root cause was a status-ordering bug: tasks were marked completed before async write confirmation, with no success check (team-notes.md, system-metrics.txt latency data)
v2.3.2 hotfix reverted the async optimization and restored write-ack-first ordering (deployment-log.md, team-notes.md)
Pilot Bank A was affected with at least 8 empty compliance reports (customer-email.txt, tickets.json T-2003)
The incident was resolved and error rates returned to baseline by 11:45 UTC (deployment-log.md, system-metrics.txt)
Write confirmation latency p99 spiked from 312ms to 2100ms during the incident window (system-metrics.txt)
likely_root_cause: agent-runtime v2.3.1 reordered the task completion lifecycle — marking tasks 'completed' before the asynchronous artifact write finished, with no validation that the write actually succeeded — causing a surge of empty reports.
supporting_evidence: deployment-log.md: v2.3.1 change log — 'Optimized async task completion reporting pipeline', 'Updated status transition logic for long-running agent tasks'
system-metrics.txt: completed_without_artifact surges at 09:15 (exact deployment minute), peaks at 6.2/min, returns to baseline after 11:30 hotfix
system-metrics.txt: write_confirmation_latency_p99 rises from 312ms to 2100ms during incident window
team-notes.md: 'the new optimization reports complete first, then writes asynchronously — but there's no check that the async write actually succeeded'
customer-email.txt: Pilot Bank A reports 8+ empty compliance reports showing 'completed' but containing no data
tickets.json: T-2003 confirms customer escalation with identical details
inferences: The async optimization lacked a compensating check (retry on failure or a pending-write state) — the incomplete implementation is the proximate cause
Some async writes may have been silently failing rather than just slow, given the completed_without_artifact rate of 6.2/min
The suspiciously high total completion rate (33/min vs 24/min baseline) is consistent with tasks being prematurely counted as completed
v2.3.1 smoke tests passed but did not validate the artifact write path — a deployment validation gap
This likely constitutes an SLA breach for Pilot Bank A (8 failures in a 99.95% SLA)
unknowns: Whether any customer data was exposed or misrouted due to the failed writes
Total number of affected tasks across all customers (metrics suggest hundreds, but only Pilot Bank A's 8 are confirmed)
Whether async writes failed due to timeouts, resource contention, or outright exceptions
Customer email reports symptoms 'since around 08:00' but deployment was at 09:15 — 75-minute discrepancy unresolved
Whether the 4-of-8 nodes v2.3.1 deployment scope vs 8-of-8 hotfix creates partial-coverage questions
recommended_actions: Immediate: Audit all tasks from 09:15-11:30 UTC for missing artifacts and re-run affected tasks — potentially hundreds of tasks beyond Pilot Bank A's 8
Short-term: Gate task completion status on confirmed artifact write success, with a post-write audit check for zero-byte or missing artifacts
Medium-term: Expand canary deployment validation to include end-to-end artifact verification with a synthetic task producing a known output
information_gaps: Agent-runtime write-side logs — would confirm exact failure mode (timeout, exception, silent drop)
Per-customer task completion rates during incident window — needed for SLA breach calculation
Full list of affected task IDs — metrics show aggregates only
Post-mortem on why async optimization was designed without a write success check
Agent logs or task start timestamps to resolve the 08:00 vs 09:15 timing discrepancy in customer email
